title: The society of the South in the early republic
description: Test your skills on the development of the South from 1800 to 1848!
topic_path: The society of the South in the early republic | 4.13
The society of the South in the early republic

**Which of the following most directly contributed to the overall trend depicted in the table? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the reliance of the South on an agricultural economy
the end of the Atlantic Slave Trade
the boom of immigration in the North
the industrialization of the southern economy

**Which of the following was an important direct effect on the South based on the information in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The South did not industrialize as much as the North.
The South had strict laws on the behaviors and movement of enslaved people.
The southern economy stagnated behind the northern economy.
The southern economy transitioned away from an agrarian economy.

**Which of the following statements best explains the overall population trend shown in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the use of enslaved labor in the South to grow cash crops
the large-scale migration from European countries to the North
the spread of the abolition movement in the South
the movement west following the Louisiana Purchase

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Compare his condition with the tenants of the poor houses in the more civilized portions of Europe–look at the sick, and the old and infirm slave, on one hand, in the midst of his family and friends, under the kind superintending care of his master and mistress, and compare it with the forlorn and wretched condition of the pauper in the poorhouse. . . I turn to the political; and here I fearlessly assert that the existing relation between the two races in the South, against which these blind fanatics are waging war, forms the most solid and durable foundation on which to rear free and stable political institutions. . . The condition of society in the South exempts us from the disorders and dangers resulting from this conflict; and which explains why it is that the political condition of the slaveholding States has been so much more stable and quiet than that of the North. . .” 

>-Source: John C. Calhoun, “Slavery as a Positive Good,” [Teaching American History,](http://teachingamericanhistory.org/library/document/slavery-a-positive-good/) 1837

**Which of the following statements best summarizes Calhoun’s argument about the difference between wage labor and enslaved labor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Enslaved people in the South were more cared for than the wage workers in the North and in Europe.
Enslaved people in the South were healthier than the wage workers in the North and in Europe.
Enslaved people in the South had more freedoms than the wage worker in the North and in Europe.
Enslaved people in the South were more devout and more literate than the wage workers in the North and in Europe.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Compare his condition with the tenants of the poor houses in the more civilized portions of Europe–look at the sick, and the old and infirm slave, on one hand, in the midst of his family and friends, under the kind superintending care of his master and mistress, and compare it with the forlorn and wretched condition of the pauper in the poorhouse. . . I turn to the political; and here I fearlessly assert that the existing relation between the two races in the South, against which these blind fanatics are waging war, forms the most solid and durable foundation on which to rear free and stable political institutions. . . The condition of society in the South exempts us from the disorders and dangers resulting from this conflict; and which explains why it is that the political condition of the slaveholding States has been so much more stable and quiet than that of the North. . .” 

>-Source: John C. Calhoun, “Slavery as a Positive Good,” [Teaching American History,](http://teachingamericanhistory.org/library/document/slavery-a-positive-good/) 1837

**Which of the following statements best describes the major change in Southerners’ views on slavery as described in the excerpt?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Southerners saw slavery as a righteous practice in which enslavers were caring for the people they enslaved.
Southerners saw slavery as a disgraceful practice but necessary for the economy of the United States.
Southerners saw slavery as a temporary practice that would only exist as long as the United States had an agricultural economy.
Southerners saw slavery as an evil that needed to be exterminated to save the morality of the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Compare his condition with the tenants of the poor houses in the more civilized portions of Europe–look at the sick, and the old and infirm slave, on one hand, in the midst of his family and friends, under the kind superintending care of his master and mistress, and compare it with the forlorn and wretched condition of the pauper in the poorhouse. . . I turn to the political; and here I fearlessly assert that the existing relation between the two races in the South, against which these blind fanatics are waging war, forms the most solid and durable foundation on which to rear free and stable political institutions. . . The condition of society in the South exempts us from the disorders and dangers resulting from this conflict; and which explains why it is that the political condition of the slaveholding States has been so much more stable and quiet than that of the North. . .” 

>-Source: John C. Calhoun, “Slavery as a Positive Good,” [Teaching American History,](http://teachingamericanhistory.org/library/document/slavery-a-positive-good/) 1837

**The excerpt would be most useful to historians as a source of information about which of the following? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the role that slavery played in crafting a distinctive regional identity
the influence of African culture on southern identity
the harsh realities of life for enslaved people in the South
the nature of gender roles within enslaved communities

assessment_items.question_content:
> “This unfortunate condition of the planters of the South has not been entirely the result of natural causes. The present crisis under which they labor, and which has been brought about by that unerring law that regulates the exchangeable commodities of all the commercial world, most clearly demonstrates that the culture of cotton as the great staple of our country, and the chief source of our national and individual wealth, engendered an inordinate eagerness to devote all the available agricultural labor of the South to its production; and the large surplus of which we now complain, the rapid accumulation of a few brief years, also conclusively demonstrates that the profits realized from its cultivation far out-stripped and defied the competition of any other agricultural staple.” 

>-Source: James Dunwoody Brownson De Bow, *De Bow’s Review,* Volume 1, Issue 3,  [University of Michigan,](https://quod.lib.umich.edu/m/moajrnl/acg1336.1-01.003/200?node=acg1336.1-01.003:2&view=pdf&size=100) 1846 

**De Bow most likely wrote his account for which of the following reasons?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To inform the public of southern agricultural practices
To persuade southern states to industrialize like the North
To encourage southern states to secede from the Union
To advocate for the end of slavery in the South

assessment_items.question_content:
> “This unfortunate condition of the planters of the South has not been entirely the result of natural causes. The present crisis under which they labor, and which has been brought about by that unerring law that regulates the exchangeable commodities of all the commercial world, most clearly demonstrates that the culture of cotton as the great staple of our country, and the chief source of our national and individual wealth, engendered an inordinate eagerness to devote all the available agricultural labor of the South to its production; and the large surplus of which we now complain, the rapid accumulation of a few brief years, also conclusively demonstrates that the profits realized from its cultivation far out-stripped and defied the competition of any other agricultural staple.” 

>-Source: James Dunwoody Brownson De Bow, *De Bow’s Review,* Volume 1, Issue 3,  [University of Michigan,](https://quod.lib.umich.edu/m/moajrnl/acg1336.1-01.003/200?node=acg1336.1-01.003:2&view=pdf&size=100) 1846 

**Which of the following was the most direct effect of conditions such as those described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the growth of a distinctive southern identity that was dependent on the production of cash crops
a lagging southern economy that pushed the Southern states to industrialize and build infrastructure
the movement of Southerners to industrialized cities in the North to find work after the southern economy failed
a push in the South to end trading with Europe because of their tariffs and find trading partners in Asia

assessment_items.question_content:
> “This unfortunate condition of the planters of the South has not been entirely the result of natural causes. The present crisis under which they labor, and which has been brought about by that unerring law that regulates the exchangeable commodities of all the commercial world, most clearly demonstrates that the culture of cotton as the great staple of our country, and the chief source of our national and individual wealth, engendered an inordinate eagerness to devote all the available agricultural labor of the South to its production; and the large surplus of which we now complain, the rapid accumulation of a few brief years, also conclusively demonstrates that the profits realized from its cultivation far out-stripped and defied the competition of any other agricultural staple.” 

>-Source: James Dunwoody Brownson De Bow, *De Bow’s Review,* Volume 1, Issue 3,  [University of Michigan,](https://quod.lib.umich.edu/m/moajrnl/acg1336.1-01.003/200?node=acg1336.1-01.003:2&view=pdf&size=100) 1846 

**Which of the following was a major difference in economic development between the North and the South in the 1830s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The North had a mixed economy, while the South had an agricultural economy.
The North used cash-crop farming, while the South practiced subsistence farming practices.
The North relied on foreign trade, while the South relied on domestic trade.
The North depended on enslaved labor, while the South depended on free labor.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9ff944cbc15bbe598cbbd15d013e184.svg)

**The pattern depicted in the map resulted most directly from which of the following factors?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: over-cultivation of land in the South
rise of utopian communities in the Midwest
decreasing foreign trade following the War of 1812
lack of manufacturing jobs in the North

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9ff944cbc15bbe598cbbd15d013e184.svg)

**Which of the following was a significant long-term result of the major pattern depicted on the map ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the spread of slavery to land in the west
the emergence of mixed-American and indigenous communities
the lack of people who identified as religious in the Midwest
the extension of suffrage to women in midwestern states

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9ff944cbc15bbe598cbbd15d013e184.svg)

**The major pattern on the map best supports which of the following statements?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: With little land available in the South, farmers looking to grow their farms moved past the Appalachian Mountains to find fertile land.
With southern states banning slavery, plantation owner moved past the Appalachian Mountains to continue enslaving people.
With massive migration from Ireland and Germany, most immigrants relocated to the Midwest for cheap and fertile land.
With the creation of new transcontinental railroads, people relocated to the Midwest to boomtowns along the railroads.
